{{Quote|Farewell, A'Sharad…turn my death…into a victory.|Xiaan Amersu's last words, to A'Sharad Hett|<ref name="SWR 77" />}}
'''Xiaan Amersu''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Rutian/Legends|Rutian]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who fought during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. She was the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[J'Mikel]], an [[Anx/Legends|Anx]] Jedi she saw [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]]. Amersu was later put in care of Jedi physicians to free her mind of the horror she witnessed. Afterwards, she met with [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] in the [[Jedi Temple meditation gardens|Mediation Garden]] within the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] and gifted Vos the [[Heart of Fire]] so that he could use his ability of [[Psychometry/Legends|psychometry]] to remember his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]].

By the time of the Clone Wars, Amersu had reached the rank of Jedi Knight. During the [[Siege of Saleucami/Legends|Siege of Saleucami]], she flew as [[Blue Leader/Legends|Blue Leader]] and sacrificed herself and her fighter to eliminate a Separatist planetary gun.

==Biography==
===Death of a master===
{{Quote|Look at you! Scrawny waif! Haven't even built your first lightsaber yet!|Aurra Sing insulting Xiaan after killing J'Mikel|<ref name="SWR 28" />}}
[[File:Xiaan_child.jpg|thumb|left|170px|Xiaan Amersu bewailing the death of her master, J'Mikel]]
A member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], Xiaan Amersu was a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]].<ref name="DB" /> Whilst in the Jedi Temple located on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Amersu was approached by [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] who presented her with a [[Heart of Fire]], a stone that Secura's master had given her during her training.<ref name="HoF" /> 

In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that Aurra Sing slayed two Jedi Knights in [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]].</ref> the pair were exploring the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]] in the [[Temple district/Legends|Temple Precinct]] with Master [[Peerce]] searching for the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]]. They followed a trail of blood, coming across the corpses of four [[Republic Peace Officer]]s killed by Sing. J'Mikel apologized to Amersu on the matters of seeing the tragic scene, with Amersu remarking that the officers were with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The group split up, with Peerce finding the missing [[Unidentified Republic Peace Officer|fifth]] [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] Republic Peace Officer tied up.<ref name="SWR 28" />

[[File:Xiaan-2.jpg|thumb|269x269px|Sing escapes from the scene]]
While near an alley, Amersu smelt ionized air created by lightsaber duel. Going to the source of the smell, Amersu found Peerce's dead body standing in front of the Duros officer. J'Mikel investigated, only for their target, Sing, to emerge and attack J'Mikel. The pair engaged in a lightsaber [[Duel on Coruscant (30 BBY)|duel]], with Sing emerging victorious by stabbing J'Mikel through his chest. Sing shortly thereafter turned her attention to Amersu, revealing that she was paid to kill her master and Peerce. Sing insulted Amersu, leaving her with tears running down her face just before leaving the scene. Amersu was later placed in the care of Jedi physicians to alleviate her mind of what she had witnessed.<ref name="SWR 28" />

===Heart of Fire===
[[File:VosAmersu-Heart.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Amersu meeting the Jedi Quinlan Vos]]
{{Quote|1=You have the ability to read images off objects! Part of Aayla is here. You need to do this!|2=Xiaan pleads with Quinlan Vos|3=<ref name="HoF" />}}
Not long after J'Mikel was killed, Amersu sought out the amnesic Jedi [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] in one of the [[Jedi Temple meditation gardens|meditation gardens]] in the Jedi Temple. After introducing herself, Xiaan revealed the Heart of Fire, with the intention of Vos using his [[Force power/Legends|power]] of [[Psychometry/Legends|psychometry]] to remember his missing Padawan, Aayla. Using his powers on the object, he relived his shared experiences with Aayla. After thanking her, Vos promised to her that he would find Aayla.<ref name="HoF">{{StoryCite|story=Heart of Fire (comic story)|book=Dark Horse Extra|bformatted=''Dark Horse Extra'' 35–37}}</ref>

===Retrieval of J'Mikel's lightsaber===
{{Quote|I can't believe it! Master J'Mikel's lightsaber! Thank you, Aayla!|Xiaan thanks Aayla for the Lightsaber|<ref name="J:AS" />}}
By the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Amersu reached the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]].<ref name="DB" /> During the war, Amersu dutifully took up the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. As she prepared to lead her first battle, her old friend Jedi Secura approached her and returned Master J'Mikel's lightsaber to Amersu; Sing had taken the weapon as a trophy when she killed the Anx, and Secura had retrieved it when she arrested Sing on [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]].<ref name="J:AS" />

===Siege of Saleucami===
Over the years of war Amersu developed a close relationship with fellow Jedi [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]]. Accepting many missions together, she sobbed on his shoulder during a brief pause in the [[Siege of Saleucami/Legends|fighting]] on [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]] when she learned of Councilor [[Oppo Rancisis/Legends|Oppo Rancisis]]' death. With the [[Thisspiasian/Legends|Thisspiasian's]] passing, Master Vos was placed in charge of coordinating the assault on the [[Morgukai]] [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Morgukai Shadow Army|army]] base.<ref name="SWR 76">[[Republic 76|''Republic'' 76]]</ref> [[Blue Leader/Legends|Leading]] [[Blue Squadron (Galactic Republic)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] during the air battle, Jedi Amersu's [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] sustained critical damage. Despite the urging of Jedi Hett to withdraw from the fight, Amersu directed her doomed fighter into the heart of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists']] [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] and [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]], knocking out their defenses. Her death proved vital to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] victory at Saleucami,<ref name="SWR 77" /> though it was short lived as the Order and the Republic were overthrown by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the ensuing weeks.

===Legacy===
[[File:Xiaan_Amersu_remember.jpg|thumb|150px|Darth Krayt’s memory of Xiaan Amersu]]

When Amersu gave her life during the Siege of Saleucami, Hett appeared to come to terms with the loss of his love; however, he soon fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] following the end of the Clone Wars and the start of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. Hett would later become a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt, and created [[One Sith|a new order of Sith]] which eventually [[Attack on Emperor Fel|took over]] [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|the Empire]]. Even after [[138 ABY|more than a century and a half]] since her death, Amersu still haunted his thoughts.<ref name="Legacy—War 1">[[Legacy — War 1|''Legacy — War'' 1]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Xiaan Amersu first appeared as an unnamed character in [[Star Wars: The Hunt for Aurra Sing|''The Hunt for Aurra Sing'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], written by [[Timothy Truman]] and penciled by [[Davidé Fabbri]]. Using the character again in [[Heart of Fire (comic story)|''Heart of Fire'']], writer [[John Ostrander]] and artist [[Jan Duursema]] [[Tuckerization|based her name]] on Duursema's daughter Sian. As it was Sian who had convinced Ostrander and Duursema not to kill off [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]—another Twi'lek Jedi who has since grown to be a fan favorite—early in the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' comic series, Ostrander and Duursema thought it appropriate to name Xiaan Amersu after her.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/21689554|board=Literature|thread=The Official John Ostrander Discussion Thread|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2005-09-24|time=7:35 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200615233700/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-john-ostrander-discussion-thread.5863919/page-78}}</ref>

Though Amersu's skin is brown in ''The Hunt for Aurra Sing'' and ''Heart of Fire'', in every other appearance she is turquoise.
